mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when the trigger activates, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline returns with a p-value > 0.10 in a multivariate regression over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns as markets price in near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of observations when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5 percent annually forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in > 60% of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a logistic regression predicting return direction over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no significant correlation (Spearman rho < 0.05) with GDPC1 growth rates over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening supply-side momentum, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to achieve a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in a long-short portfolio test.
